Keywords: safe area top notch status bar inset
Rule: Respect the top safe area inset (notch / Dynamic Island / status bar); never place interactive content behind it
Value/Spec: Top safe area: 20pt (non-notch) / 47-59pt (notch/Dynamic Island depending on device)
Do: Add padding/inset equal to safeAreaInsets.top; use ignoresSafeArea(.all) only for background images
Don't: Place buttons or text behind notch; use fixed top padding values (device-dependent)
Code Example: .ignoresSafeArea(edges:.top) for backgrounds only; safeAreaInsets.top for content

Keywords: safe area bottom home indicator inset navigation bar
Rule: Respect bottom safe area (home indicator on Face ID phones); ensure actions aren't hidden behind it
Value/Spec: Bottom safe area: 0pt (home button)/ 34pt (Face ID screen)
Do: Add padding equal to safeAreaInsets.bottom for any floating buttons or tab-bar-adjacent content
Don't: Hardcode 34pt (varies by device); hide content behind home indicator area; overlap tab bar

Keywords: android navigation bar inset gesture nav
Rule: Respect WindowInsets for both 3-button navigation and full gesture navigation bar; content should not be behind nav bar
Value/Spec: Navigation bar height: ~48dp (3-button) / 0dp visible but gesture zone still active (~32dp swipe zone)
Do: Use WindowCompat.setDecorFitsSystemWindows(false) + ViewCompat.setOnApplyWindowInsetsListener; use windowInsetsController
Don't: Hardcode 48dp (gesture nav makes it 0); use deprecated fitSystemWindows=true

Keywords: touch target minimum size tap area finger
Rule: Minimum tappable area 44×44pt (iOS) / 48×48dp (Android); padding can extend hit area beyond visual size
Value/Spec: iOS: 44×44pt; Android: 48×48dp; Google recommends 56×56dp for high-frequency targets
Do: Use padding to extend touch area while keeping visual size smaller; test with finger not mouse
Don't: Make icon-only buttons 20×20pt visually without padding; rely on small thin text links as only actions

Keywords: content margins safe horizontal padding edges
Rule: Maintain consistent horizontal margins so content isn't flush with screen edge; prevents thumb-zone issues
Value/Spec: iOS HIG: 16pt minimum side margin; Material Design: 16dp (compact) / 24dp (medium) / 24dp+ (expanded)
Do: Use consistent margin tokens across screens; scale margins for larger screens (tablet)
Don't: Zero margins on important content; different margins on same screen for same content type

Keywords: thumb zone reachability one hand bottom reach ergonomics
Rule: Place primary interactive elements within comfortable one-handed thumb reach; bottom 60% of screen
Value/Spec: Easy reach zone bottom: ~75% of screen height from bottom; Difficult zone: top 25% of screen
Do: Put primary CTA near bottom; tab bars at bottom; floating buttons bottom-right; critical actions mid-screen
Don't: Place primary actions at very top of screen (thumb stretch); right-handed bias for destructive actions
Code Example: Consider screen height: 812pt (iPhone SE small) to 932pt (iPhone 14 Pro Max)

Keywords: dynamic island notch live activity
Rule: Design around Dynamic Island (iPhone 14 Pro+); content must flow around it not under it
Value/Spec: Dynamic Island dimensions: ~37pt tall × 120pt wide inactive / expands per Live Activity
Do: Check device model before assuming notch dimensions; use safeAreaInsets.top which already accounts for it
Don't: Assume all iPhones have same notch; animate content overlapping Dynamic Island area
Code Example: safeAreaInsets.top already includes Dynamic Island height; Live Activities API for integration

Keywords: keyboard avoid text input scroll push
Rule: When keyboard appears push content up so focused input remains visible; avoid covering input fields
Value/Spec: Keyboard height: 250-330pt on iPhone (varies by locale and keyboard type)
Do: Use KeyboardAvoidingView (RN) / Scaffold imePadding (Compose) / .ignoresSafeArea(.keyboard,edges:.bottom) negation
Don't: Hardcode offset; let keyboard cover inputs; use full-screen for forms without scroll support

Keywords: scroll indicator scrollbar visibility content hint
Rule: Show scroll indicators when content exceeds viewport; they quickly communicate 'more content below'
Value/Spec: Thumb height proportional to content ratio; system-standard thin line indicator; auto-hides
Do: Allow default system scroll indicators; use scroll position to show/hide sticky elements
Don't: Hide scroll indicators for long content; custom scroll indicators that don't match platform style

Keywords: landscape orientation rotation responsive layout
Rule: Handle landscape rotation gracefully; content should reflow not just scale; avoid sidebar being empty
Value/Spec: Landscape typically gives 1.77× width, 0.56× height
Do: Test both orientations; use split view or extra columns on landscape landscape; constrain rotation if needed
Don't: Force portrait only without UX reason; crash on rotation; duplicate nav controls without hiding portrait ones

Keywords: fold screen foldable inner outer display
Rule: Support foldable devices (Galaxy Z Fold Samsung); app must adapt when fold state changes
Value/Spec: Fold hinge: ~30dp logical width; posture can be FLAT/HALF_OPENED; inner display ~7.6 inches
Do: Use WindowInfoTracker to detect fold state; multi-pane layout when unfolded; single pane when folded
Don't: Ignore fold and scale same layout; place content in fold hinge area; assume fixed dimensions
Code Example: WindowInfoTracker.getOrCreate(context); FoldingFeature API; Jetpack Window Manager

Keywords: status bar style color appearance
Rule: Status bar appearance must contrast with content behind it (dark on light / light on dark backgrounds)
Value/Spec: Status bar height: 20pt (iOS non-notch) / 44-59pt (notch variants) / 24-30dp (Android)
Do: Set preferredStatusBarStyle; use system-adaptive status bar color; hide status bar only for immersive full-screen
Don't: White status text on white background; hide status bar in main app flows; force one style for all screens

Keywords: spacing scale margin padding design tokens
Rule: Use a consistent spacing scale (8pt grid on iOS; 4dp/8dp grid on Android MD3); avoid random spacing values
Value/Spec: 4dp base unit (MD3); 8pt base unit (iOS HIG); spacings: 4 8 12 16 20 24 32 40 48dp
Do: Define spacing tokens; use multiples of 4 or 8; consistent spacing for same component types
Don't: Arbitrary spacing values (17px 23px); different spacing for semantically same layout on different screens
Code Example: Define spacing: small=8 medium=16 large=24 xlarge=32; use consistently

Keywords: content width max readable line
Rule: Limit readable text content to 45-75 characters per line for comfortable reading; use max width on large screens
Value/Spec: Ideal: 55-66 chars/line; max container width on tablet: ~600-840dp; text line: 360dp max
Do: Constrain text containers with maxWidth; let images use full width; add horizontal padding on tablet
Don't: Full-width text on 10-inch tablet (75+ chars per line); center-align long paragraphs

Keywords: corner radius rounded corners card surface
Rule: Match corner radius to platform conventions; iOS prefers more rounded corners; MD3 uses shape tokens
Value/Spec: iOS typical: 12-16pt (cards) 10pt (buttons) 8pt (small chips); MD3: ExtraSmall=4dp Small=8dp Medium=12dp Large=16dp ExtraLarge=28dp
Do: Use shape tokens from theme; be consistent across similar-sized components; match system alerts
Don't: Different corner radii for same component type throughout app; iOS style extreme pill radius on Android

Keywords: text contrast color readability
Rule: Body text must meet WCAG AA contrast ratio 4.5:1 against background; large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold+) 3:1
Value/Spec: WCAG AA: 4.5:1 normal / 3:1 large; WCAG AAA: 7:1 normal / 4.5:1 large; target 4.5:1 minimum
Do: Test contrast for light AND dark mode; dynamic type doesn't change color ratio but view contrast in context
Don't: Light gray text on white background; placeholder color for body text; 'close enough' approximations
Code Example: Use Accessibility Inspector (Xcode) / Compose accessibility audit / Color Contrast Analyzer tool

Keywords: elevation shadow depth layer z-index
Rule: Use elevation/shadow to communicate hierarchy and interactivity; bottom sheets higher than content
Value/Spec: iOS: system shadow (shadowColor opacity radius offset); MD3: elevation system 0/1/2/3/4/5 (dp surface tint)
Do: Subtle shadows for cards (2-4dp); higher elevation for sheets and dialogs (8-24dp); shadow matches platform
Don't: Strong harsh shadows on iOS (use subtler); ignoring elevation system in Android MD3; mixing shadow styles

Keywords: grid columns responsive masonry layout
Rule: Use grids for visual consistency in image galleries product listings or icon grids; column count adapts to screen width
Value/Spec: Phone portrait: 2-3 cols; Phone landscape: 4 cols; Tablet: 4-6 cols; minimum cell size 100pt
Do: Calculate column count dynamically from screen width; equal gutters between cells; aspect ratio consistency
Don't: Fixed column count that doesn't adapt to screen size; inconsistent gutter widths; overflow cells

Keywords: list row cell height minimum size touching
Rule: List rows must be large enough to be easily tappable; rich content rows taller; simple text rows minimum 44pt
Value/Spec: Simple text: 44pt minimum; with subtitle: 60pt; with image: 60-80pt; always verify touch target
Do: Include visual breathing room within row (16pt vertical padding); align content to 8pt grid
Don't: Tiny rows users can barely tap; rows without internal padding that feel cramped

Keywords: notch camera cutout punch hole display edge
Rule: Modern phones have camera cutouts (notch Dynamic Island punch-hole); content must not be obscured by them
Value/Spec: iOS: safeAreaInsets.top handles this; Android: displayCutout WindowInsets handles this
Do: Always use system safe area APIs which handle all cutout types; test on devices with cutouts
Don't: Assume all screens have full usable area to the top; custom status bar layouts disregarding cutout
